mathml_representations_list:
<mstyle id="custom-group-0" class="custom-group"><mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_85">σ</mi>
          <mrow>
            <mo>(</mo>
            <mrow>
              <mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_86">u</mi>
            </mrow>
            <mo>)</mo>
          </mrow>
          </mstyle>

<mstyle id="custom-group-1" class="custom-group"><mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_87">ε</mi>
          <mrow>
            <mo>(</mo>
            <mrow>
              <mi mathvariant="bold-italic" id="mi_88">v</mi>
            </mrow>
            <mo>)</mo>
          </mrow>
          </mstyle>

concepts_list.name: sigma_u
concepts_list.description: Cauchy stress tensor evaluated at the displacement field u
concepts_list.justification: The symbol σ(u) represents the stress tensor derived from the displacement field u, as defined in the text: "σ = Cε(u)" and used in the weak form of elastostatics.

concepts_list.name: epsilon_v
concepts_list.description: Linearized strain tensor evaluated at the test function v
concepts_list.justification: The symbol ε(v) is defined as the linearized strain tensor of the test function v, given by the formula: ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T), as stated in the text.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Volume differential element in the domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΩ appears in multiple integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Ω, and is used to denote the volume element in the domain.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Surface differential element on the boundary Γ
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΓ appears in boundary integrals over Γ, such as ∫_Γ, and denotes the surface element on the domain boundary.

concepts_list.name: t_bar
concepts_list.description: Prescribed traction vector on the boundary Γ_t
concepts_list.justification: The symbol t̄ is introduced as the prescribed traction on the Neumann boundary Γ_t, used in the weak form of the elastostatics problem.

concepts_list.name: v
concepts_list.description: Test function in the variational formulation
concepts_list.justification: The symbol v appears multiple times as a test function in the weak form, such as in ∫_Ω v f dΩ and ∫_Γ v t̄ dΓ, and is used in the Galerkin method.

concepts_list.name: V
concepts_list.justification: The symbol V is introduced as the function space for test functions, with v ∈ V, and is used in the weak form and discretization.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.justification: The symbol Ω is repeatedly used to denote the domain of interest, which is a region in R^d, and is the domain over which the volume integrals are defined.

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.justification: The symbol u is used as the primary unknown in both thermal and elastostatic problems, and is referred to as the primal variable in the abstract form of the problem.

concepts_list.name: b
concepts_list.justification: The symbol b is introduced as the body force in the elastostatics problem, appearing in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v ⋅ b dΩ.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary where traction is prescribed
concepts_list.justification: The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the boundary integral involving the traction t̄.

new_concept_info.description:
Cauchy stress tensor evaluated at the displacement field u
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol σ(u) represents the stress tensor derived from the displacement field u, as defined in the text: "σ = Cε(u)" and used in the weak form of elastostatics.

Linearized strain tensor evaluated at the test function v
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol ε(v) is defined as the linearized strain tensor of the test function v, given by the formula: ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T), as stated in the text.

Volume differential element in the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΩ appears in multiple integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Ω, and is used to denote the volume element in the domain.

Surface differential element on the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΓ appears in boundary integrals over Γ, such as ∫_Γ, and denotes the surface element on the domain boundary.

Prescribed traction vector on the boundary Γ_t
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol t̄ is introduced as the prescribed traction on the Neumann boundary Γ_t, used in the weak form of the elastostatics problem.

Test function in the variational formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol v appears multiple times as a test function in the weak form, such as in ∫_Ω v f dΩ and ∫_Γ v t̄ dΓ, and is used in the Galerkin method.

Function space of test functions
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol V is introduced as the function space for test functions, with v ∈ V, and is used in the weak form and discretization.

Main computational domain
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Ω is repeatedly used to denote the domain of interest, which is a region in R^d, and is the domain over which the volume integrals are defined.

Primal variable (displacement or temperature field)
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol u is used as the primary unknown in both thermal and elastostatic problems, and is referred to as the primal variable in the abstract form of the problem.

Body force vector in elastostatics
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol b is introduced as the body force in the elastostatics problem, appearing in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v ⋅ b dΩ.

Neumann boundary where traction is prescribed
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the boundary integral involving the traction t̄.
